methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kvertus is a growth-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Yaroslav Filimonov and Andrii Znaichenko, currently raising capital on ~$130M total raised. The comparable universe consists of 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies, including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this valuation. While 20 same-sector Defense & Security peers exist in the broader market, rigorous filtering for stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints—the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. Applying a broad sector-wide median would introduce unacceptable noise, given the heterogeneity in business models, growth profiles, and go-to-market trajectories within Defense & Security.

This analysis therefore cannot derive a valuation range from quantitative comparables at this time. Proceeding with a defensible valuation would require either: (i) relaxing stage-specificity constraints and documenting the adjustment rationale explicitly, (ii) applying alternative methods (discounted cash flow, revenue multiple analysis) if forward-looking financial data becomes available, or (iii) broadening the comp set to adjacent sectors with acceptable methodological transparency. Without sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers, quantitative triangulation remains unreliable, and valuation work should be deferred pending additional market data or alternative analytical approaches.
